data.title: Two Years to Unemployment: Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Notches Up With New Projection of AI in White Collar Work
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) just started one doozy of a countdown clock for anyone who works in an office setting, as it projects that artificial intelligence (AI) will automate most white-collar work, from lawyers to accountants and beyond, in somewhere around 12 to 18 months. But that was not the only prediction Microsoft brought out about AI. While Microsoft is regarding one of the greatest sea changes in the economy since the arrival of the internet as a thing of the very near future, investors were still reasonably pleased. Microsoft shares ticked up fractionally in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft's Mustafa Suleyman noted that AI is reaching a point where "…most professional white-collar tasks" could be automated in the next two years, as AI has reached "…human-level performance" of most of its roles. This is actually being seen at Microsoft itself, as Microsoft software engineers are using "…AI-assisted coding" for large parts of their overall coding.

And while this sounds like a countdown to doomsday, in which all those employees are replaced by machines, which kicks off a dystopian nightmare of persistently high unemployment, this may not be the case at all. Suleyman also looks for a "major AI safety incident" to take place in the next two to three years without some kind of large-scale regulatory mechanism in place to prevent such things.

"More Flexible, Connected Future"

And for those whose jobs will be lost to AI in the near future, Microsoft has a plan waiting for you there, too. It is working to build a "…more flexible, connected future" for the Xbox platform, which it expects to reveal soon. With this year being the 25th anniversary of Xbox, it is little surprise that Microsoft has some big announcements to come.

The Game Developers Conference kicks off next month in San Francisco, and with its arrival, Microsoft will have a range of panels waiting to show off the future as it sees it. This includes one panel called "Building for the Future with Xbox," which will likely include a portion about why the next Xbox is really just a glorified PC now.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.61% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $593.38 per share implies 46.67% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 2/13/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher to close out the week following an encouraging inflation report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 0.2% in January, below the consensus estimate of 0.3%. On an annual basis, CPI increased by 2.4%, below the estimate of 2.5% and marking the slowest pace of growth since May 2025.

Investors Eye Pentagon List and Steel, Aluminum Tariffs

Elsewhere, the Pentagon published a document this morning showing that Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), and BYD (BYDDY), along with several other companies, were added to the Section 1260H list. The list highlights firms that the Pentagon believes are associated with China's military. However, the document was later taken down for unknown reasons. That led some to believe that the U.S. seeks to maintain a steady relationship with China ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in April.

Furthermore, the market reacted positively to reports that the Trump administration would scale back its 50% tariffs on some steel and aluminum products.  However, White House trade adviser Peter Navarro disputed the reports and emphasized that Trump would maintain a "no exemptions, no exclusions" stance on the tariffs.

data.description:

data.title: "Now-Constant Stream of Experienced Talent Leaving Tesla": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Gains Despite Fresh Departure
data.description:
It has been something of a difficult time to be part of the institutional knowledge at electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA). Several long-term employees, many of whom are higher up in the organizational structure, have departed the company recently. One more just joined the roster of the departed, although investors are not particularly unnerved by this. Tesla shares ticked up fractionally in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New reports note that the Director of Vehicle Operations and Engineering at Fremont, Benjamin Bate, has departed the company after eight years of service. Reports suggest that there has been a common thread among the departed, with employees showing "…a growing discontent with the automaker following the company's shift toward robotics and artificial intelligence."

Model manufacturing leads, sales executives and vehicle designers have all jumped ship in recent days, and employees are increasingly losing interest in their work. One report noted, "Tesla is no longer innovating or pushing the envelope in automobiles." This is, to an extent, true, as Tesla has not brought out a new model since the Cybertruck, and the Roadster is still something of a gray area.

US Sales in Open Decline

Meanwhile, new sales numbers hit recently for January, and suggest that maybe all the departed Tesla employees had a point after all. Tesla's United States sales figures for January 2026 were down 17% against the same time in 2025, reports noted.

Tesla sold around 40,100 vehicles for the month, which was a significant departure from the 48,500 it sold in January 2025. While Tesla does not report those numbers itself, third-party registration figures help cover that particular gap. These numbers are concerning for another reason, as reports note that this is the fourth month in a row sales have been down.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.21% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5.46% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Fed's Miran is 'Unconcerned about Inflation,' Urges Rate Cuts
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran reiterated his stance on lowering interest rates, arguing that a strong jobs report for January doesn't weaken the case for easing monetary policy while inflation remains under control.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"As long as I remain unconcerned about inflation, I think it makes sense to continue trying to underwrite the labor market with looser monetary policy, particularly as supply expands more than demand and the economy can grow without generating inflation," Miran said at a Dallas Fed event.

Inflation Slows Ahead of March FOMC Meeting

Miran's statement was put to the test when the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published January's Consumer Price Index (CPI) this morning, showing inflation rising by 2.4% annually. That marked the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. In addition, core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 2.5% from the prior year, registering its lowest level since March 2021.

Miran has voted in favor of rate cuts in every Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting since he joined the board in September. The next meeting is scheduled for March 18.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rallies on Cooling Inflation as Pentagon Withdraws Updated 1260H List
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Friday following an encouraging inflation reading from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS).

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Consumer Price Index (CPI) for January rose by 0.2% month-over-month, below the consensus estimate of 0.3%. It increased by 2.4% on an annual basis, below the expectation of 2.5% and registering the slowest pace of growth since May 2025. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 0.3% from the prior month and 2.5% annually, both of which were in line with their estimates.

"Headline CPI inflation was a touch softer than expected in January, delivering a welcome surprise to the downside at the beginning of the year," wrote Oxford Economics lead economist Bernard Yaros in a note.

This morning, the Pentagon published a document detailing the inclusion of Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), Nio (NIO), and several other companies to the Section 1260H list. The list flags Chinese companies the Pentagon views as having ties to the country's military. However, the document has since been taken down from the Pentagon's website, with some attributing the move to the U.S. seeking to ease tensions before the Trump-Xi summit in April.

Elsewhere, the Financial Times reported that the Trump administration is planning to exempt some steel and aluminum products from tariffs in light of affordability concerns, which have affected products like drink cans and pie tins. Since the tariffs were imposed last June, the price of aluminum has risen by 24%, while steel has edged higher by about 4%. However, White House trade adviser Peter Navarro later denied the report, saying that Trump will maintain a "no exemptions, no exclusions" approach.

Earlier this week, a study from the New York Fed found that Americans absorb 90% of the increased costs from tariffs. The average tariff rate on U.S. imports has risen to 13% from 2.6%, with importers passing on higher costs to consumers.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.50% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Uranium ETFs Slow Down Heading Into the Weekend
data.description:
Uranium exchange-traded funds (ETFs) slowed down on Friday, experiencing only slight movements, as investors prepare for the long holiday weekend.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Even so, there's still some nuclear energy news that investors will want to read before they clock out for the weekend. Let's dig into that below:

  • Russian officials have challenged the U.S. government's plans to build nuclear power plants in Armenia, as it falls within what Russia believes is its area of influence.
  • The Perry Nuclear Power Plant in Ohio has moved to extend operations into 2060 following a deal to power Meta Platforms' (META) artificial intelligence (AI) data centers.
  • India's Rajasthan Atomic Power Project Unit-7 reached full power for the first time this week, generating 700 MW. This is the third of 16 nuclear energy units planned to be built in India.

Uranium ETF Stock Movements Today

Uranium ETFs experienced minimal movement on Friday.

  • Global X Uranium ETF (URA) was up 0.19% on Friday.
  • NorthShore Global Uranium Mining ETF (URNM) slipped 0.12% today.
  • Sprott Junior Uranium Miners ETF (URNJ) dipped 0.05% as of this writing.
  • VanEck Uranium + Nuclear Energy ETF (NLR) jumped 0.49% this afternoon.

Which Uranium ETF Is the Safest Bet?

Turning to the TipRanks ETF comparison tool, traders can see which of these ETFs is the best bet. URA is a favorite of nuclear energy investors and has experienced the most growth over the past 12 months at 85.45%. The ETF also has a strong dividend yield of 3.88%. URNM offers the second-best investment, with an 81.84% gain over the past year and a 2.42% dividend.
data.author: William White

data.title: U.S. Removes List Linking Alibaba, Baidu to China Military
data.description:
This morning, the Pentagon added several Chinese companies to its Section 1260H list, which identifies firms it says are linked to or supportive of China's military. The companies included Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), and BYD (BYDDY).

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the document showing the new additions has since been removed from the Pentagon's website. In its place is a message stating, "This Document Was Withdrawn."

Alibaba Challenges Addition to Section 1260H List

It's unclear why the document was taken down, although some experts speculate that President Trump didn't want to raise tensions ahead of his April summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, which could complicate trade or security negotiations.

"Alibaba is not a Chinese military company nor part of any military-civil fusion strategy," said a company spokesperson in response to its addition to the list. "We will take all available legal action against attempts to misrepresent our company."

Companies that were already on the list include Tencent (TCEHY), CATL (CYATY), and DJI.
